metadata.notes: Voice rewrite — conversational walkthrough style matching Wingspan gold standard. Sources: official rulebook, Stonemaier FAQ, BGG forums, community strategy guides.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Board and Timers
- 1 main game board with 3 action rows — each row has multiple action spaces, and every space is split into 2 columns
- 3 sand timers with different durations:
  - **Black timer** (45 seconds) — cycles fast, gives small rewards
  - **Green timer** (2 minutes) — moderate actions, moderate wait
  - **Purple timer** (3 minutes) — slowest cycle, but the most powerful actions live here
- Each action space has a "before timer" column (left) and an "after timer" column (right), separated by a slot where the timer sits

#### Player Components (per player)
- 1 asymmetric character mat (double-sided: basic on one side, advanced on the other) with a unique ability and starting resources
- 2 worker pawns: 1 regular worker + 1 grande worker (the grande can share spaces with other players' workers)
- 4 track markers for Power, Prestige, Popularity, and Legendary Achievement
- 1 VP marker

#### Cards
- **Province cards** — your engine-building cards. They provide ongoing bonuses, resource production, or end-game scoring. You tuck them under your player board organized by action color.
- **Stratagem cards** — 4 unique cards per character side (40 total across all 10 character sides). These are one-time or ongoing rule-bending abilities you can play for free during the Council Phase.
- **Council Reward cards** — earned during the Rest Phase for track advancement.
- **Achievement cards** — bonus end-game scoring conditions.

#### Resources and Tokens
- Gold, vote, military strength, and prestige tokens
- Round marker (tracks rounds 1–4)
- Up to 10 influence tokens per player

#### Tracks
- 4 advancement tracks: Power, Prestige, Popularity, Legendary Achievement
- Each track has a **threshold line** — you must push all 4 markers past their thresholds into the "parchment" zone to be eligible to win

**Checkpoint:** Each player should have their character mat (basic side for first game), 2 workers, 4 track markers at starting positions, starting resources, and stratagem cards. The board should have the 3 timers in their starting positions, province cards in the display, and the round marker on Round 1.

subtopics.title: Game Structure & Core Loop
subtopics.content:
#### No Turns — Everyone Acts at Once
Pendulum has **no turns**. All players act simultaneously during the Council Phase, placing workers, flipping timers, and collecting rewards in real time. The theme: you're powerful nobles vying to succeed the Timeless King as ruler of Dunya.

#### How the Timers Work
Three sand timers sit on the board, one per action row. Each action space has two columns:
- **"Before" column (left)**: You place your worker here WHILE the timer is NOT on this row.
- **"After" column (right)**: Workers slide here when the timer arrives and gets flipped, and you can then retrieve them to collect the action's reward.

#### The Action Cycle — Step by Step
1. **Place a worker** on the "before" side of an action space on any row where the timer is NOT currently sitting.
2. **Wait** for the timer to run out and get flipped onto that row.
3. **Any player may flip** a timer once its sand runs out — flipping is voluntary and strategic. Nobody is forced to flip.
4. When flipped, ALL workers on the "before" side of that row slide to the "after" side.
5. **Retrieve your worker** from the "after" side to gain the action's reward.
6. Your worker is now free to be placed again.

#### Round Structure (4 Rounds Total)
Each round has two phases:

**1. Council Phase** — Real-time simultaneous play. This lasts until the purple timer has been flipped **3 times** (roughly 9 minutes of real time). Everyone is placing workers, flipping timers, gaining resources, and advancing tracks all at once.

**2. Rest Phase** — Sequential cleanup, step by step:
   1. Determine Privilege based on who has the most vote tokens.
   2. Gain Council Reward cards in Privilege order (1st place: first pick + 2 VP advances; 2nd/3rd: 1 VP advance).
   3. Discard down to 2 province cards per color.
   4. Reset the board with new cards and tokens.
   5. Optionally reposition your workers.

#### Key Constraints
- Regular workers: only 1 per space (exception: the black section has unlimited capacity — any number of workers can share).
- Grande worker: can always be placed regardless of who else is there.
- You CANNOT place on a row where the timer has run out and is waiting to be flipped.
- You CANNOT retrieve from the "before" side — your worker must wait for the timer to push it to "after."
- A worker sitting on "after" blocks you from placing on the "before" side of that same space until you retrieve it.

**Checkpoint:** Before starting, make sure everyone understands the before/after column system and that flipping timers is voluntary. Walk through one cycle: place on "before" → timer flips → worker slides to "after" → retrieve for reward.

subtopics.title: Detailed Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Timer Flipping Rules
- Any player may flip a timer once the sand runs out — you don't need a worker on that row to flip it.
- When flipped, ALL workers on the "before" side simultaneously slide to the "after" side.
- **Flipping is voluntary.** Nobody is forced to flip. Delaying is a legitimate strategic tool — you can stall a flip to block opponents from retrieving, or buy yourself time to place a worker before the timer moves.
- Black timer (45 seconds) cycles fastest, giving frequent small rewards. Purple timer (3 minutes) cycles slowest, gating the most powerful actions behind long waits.
- A round ends when the purple timer has been flipped 3 times total.

#### Worker Placement Rules
- **Regular worker**: Can only go on unoccupied "before" spaces (exception: the black section has unlimited capacity — multiple workers can always share).
- **Grande worker**: Can be placed on any "before" space regardless of other workers present. However, a regular worker CANNOT be placed on a space already occupied by a grande worker (unless it's in the black section).
- You must retrieve a worker from "after" before you can place that same worker again.

#### Action Spaces
Each row has multiple spaces with different effects:
- **Gain resources**: Gold, votes, military strength, prestige tokens.
- **Advance a track**: Pay required resources to push up Power, Prestige, or Popularity.
- **Acquire province cards**: Draft from the shared display into your tableau. Province cards tuck under your board by color — future activations of that color grant you the province bonus too.
- **Play stratagem cards**: Free action during Council Phase (no worker required). These bend the rules in powerful ways.
- **Convert resources**: Trade one type for another.

The row determines power level: Black = quick, small rewards. Green = moderate. Purple = most powerful but slowest to cycle.

#### Province Cards (Your Engine)
- Acquired from the shared display via specific action spaces.
- Tucked under your player board, organized by action color.
- Every time you take an action of that color, ALL province cards in that slot fire their bonuses.
- During Rest Phase cleanup: discard down to 2 per color.
- Province cards compound over 4 rounds — a card bought in Round 1 pays out roughly 12 times. A card bought in Round 4 pays out maybe 3 times.

#### Stratagem Cards
- You start with 4 unique stratagems based on your character side. Play them anytime during Council Phase as a free action.
- Effects include: extra worker placement, free track advancement, resource windfalls, moving locked workers.
- Some have timing restrictions ("play when you flip a timer," "play during Rest Phase").
- Council Reward cards with lightning bolt icons are one-time use.

#### Character Asymmetry
- 5 double-sided character mats = 10 playable characters. Basic sides play similarly; advanced sides diverge significantly.
- Each character has different track lengths for Power, Prestige, and Popularity — the distance to each threshold varies per character, creating asymmetric difficulty.
- Characters include Bolk ("The Champion" / "The Warmonger"), Licinia ("The Implacable"), Mesoat, Drenkhir, and Gambal ("The Briber").

#### Timeless Mode (Turn-Based Variant)
If real-time pressure isn't your thing, Pendulum includes a turn-based variant. Players take sequential turns: place a worker, retrieve a worker, or flip a timer. All other rules stay the same. Highly recommended for your first game.

subtopics.title: Scoring & End Game
subtopics.content:
#### Game End
After the Rest Phase of Round 4. Exactly 4 rounds — no early termination.

#### The Win Condition: Cross All 4 Thresholds
Each of the 4 tracks (Power, Prestige, Popularity, Legendary Achievement) has a threshold line dividing it into a regular zone and a "parchment" zone.

- To be **eligible to win**, ALL 4 of your markers must be past their thresholds into the parchment zone.
- If only 1 player qualifies: they win outright.
- If multiple players qualify: whoever is farthest past the thresholds in aggregate wins.
- If nobody qualifies: highest aggregate track position wins (but this rarely happens with experienced players).

This is the central strategic tension — you MUST cross all 4 thresholds. Maxing one track while neglecting another makes you ineligible.

#### Additional Scoring
- Province card end-game bonuses (based on track positions, resources, or conditions)
- Achievement card bonuses
- VP earned during the game (already tracked)
- Character-specific scoring bonuses

#### Tiebreakers
1. Compare your lowest individual track position (higher is better).
2. Highest Privilege.
3. Most remaining resources.

#### What to Expect
- First game: Focus entirely on crossing thresholds — don't worry about optimizing.
- Experienced play: All 4 thresholds crossed with province bonuses and achievements stacked on top.
- The winner is almost always someone who crossed all 4 thresholds — raw VP without threshold eligibility rarely wins.

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Learn the Timer Rhythm First
Your first game, just get comfortable with the place → wait → flip → retrieve cycle. Speed comes with practice. The black timer (45 seconds) is your training wheel — fast cycles, frequent feedback, low stakes.

#### Balance All 4 Tracks
Because you must cross ALL 4 thresholds to be eligible to win, ignoring any track is catastrophic. Always check which track is lagging and push it next. Each character has different track lengths — study your mat to know which thresholds are close and which are far.

#### Buy Province Cards Early
Province cards generate bonuses every time you take that action color. A province bought in Round 1 fires across 4 full Council Phases. Bought in Round 4, it fires maybe 3 times. Front-load your engine.

#### Don't Sleep on the Privilege Track
First place in Privilege gets first pick of Council Rewards + 2 VP advances (vs. 1 VP for 2nd/3rd place). Over 4 Rest Phases, that adds up. Accumulate vote tokens when you can.

#### Use Your Grande Worker on Contested Spaces
The grande worker ignores the 1-worker-per-space limit. Place it on high-value spaces where opponents are competing. But think twice before parking it on a purple (3-minute) space — it'll be locked there for a long time.

#### Fill Downtime with Stratagems
While waiting for timers to complete, play your stratagem cards — they're free actions. Resource generation, worker repositioning, or track advancement at zero time cost.

#### Keep Both Workers Active
While one worker waits for a timer, the other should be placing or retrieving. Never let both workers sit idle on "after" spaces simultaneously.

#### Common Mistakes
- **Timer panic** — Rushing to place without thinking. Even in real-time, 2 seconds of thought saves wasted placements.
- **Track neglect** — Maxing Power while Prestige sits at zero. Your win eligibility is gated by your weakest track.
- **Forgetting to retrieve** — Workers on "after" are locked until you pick them up. Every idle moment is a wasted opportunity.
- **Ignoring the purple row** — 3-minute wait, yes, but the most powerful actions live there. Get a worker committed early each round.
- **Late engine building** — Province cards in Rounds 3–4 barely pay off. Build your engine in Rounds 1–2.
- **Not flipping timers strategically** — Flipping is voluntary. Sometimes flip early to retrieve your worker; sometimes delay to block opponents from retrieving theirs.

#### Timeless Mode for Learning
If real-time overwhelms you, play Timeless Mode first — same mechanics, no time pressure. Switch to real-time once you've got the rhythm.

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Your First Game of Pendulum

**Setup**: Take your character mat (basic side for your first game). Place your 4 track markers at their starting positions. Take your 2 workers — 1 regular, 1 grande. Set starting resources per your character. On the board, place the 3 timers: black (45s), green (2min), purple (3min).

**Round 1, Council Phase Begins**: All timers start running simultaneously. Don't panic — breathe.

**Your First Move**: Look at the black section (45-second timer). Place your regular worker on a "before" space that gives resources you need. The black timer runs out fast — within 45 seconds, someone will flip it, and your worker slides to "after." Retrieve it to gain the reward. Done — you've completed one full cycle.

**While You Wait**: Place your grande worker on a green or purple space. The grande can go anywhere regardless of other workers. Purple spaces are powerful but slow — commit your grande there early so it's ready when the timer flips.

**The Rhythm**: Black timer flips every 45 seconds — use it for rapid small gains (resources, conversions). Green timer flips every 2 minutes — moderate rewards. Purple timer flips every 3 minutes — biggest payoff, longest wait. Cycle your regular worker through black spaces while your grande sits on a purple space.

**Track Advancement**: Spend resources to advance your Power, Prestige, Popularity, and Legendary Achievement tracks. Check your character mat — which thresholds are closest? Push those first. Remember: you need ALL 4 past the line to win.

**Province Cards**: When you can, acquire province cards from the display. Tuck them under your board by color. Every future activation of that color gives you the province bonus too — that's your engine building.

**Round Ends**: The purple timer flips for the 3rd time — Council Phase is over. Rest Phase: determine Privilege (most votes), gain Council Reward cards, clean up, optionally reposition your workers.

**Rounds 2–4**: Same structure, but your engine builds. Province cards compound, making each action more powerful. Push all 4 tracks past their threshold lines. Use stratagems for free bonuses whenever you have a moment.

**Scoring**: Cross all 4 thresholds to be eligible to win. Add province bonuses + achievement bonuses + in-game VP. First game goal: cross at least 3 of 4 thresholds and get comfortable with the timer rhythm.

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Timer Manipulation
Flipping is voluntary — and that's where advanced play lives. Delay flipping a timer when your opponent has a worker on "before" and you don't, denying them the slide to "after." Flip early when you need to retrieve before placing elsewhere. Watch the purple timer especially — controlling when it flips can give you an extra placement cycle that opponents miss.

#### Worker Specialization
Your grande worker ignores occupancy limits, making it ideal for contested high-value spaces. Your regular worker should stick to the black section (unlimited capacity, fast cycles) for reliable throughput. The grande on purple + regular cycling black is the default strong pattern. Break this only when a green-row province card is critical to your engine.

#### Province Card Prioritization
Not all province cards are equal. Cards that produce resources for free on activation compound hardest — a gold-producing province in the black section (45-second cycles) fires constantly. Cards that give end-game VP for track positions are only worth grabbing if you're already pushing those tracks. Prioritize: resource production > track advancement bonuses > end-game conditions.

#### The 4-Threshold Map
Each character's 4 tracks have different lengths. Bolk's Power track is short (easy threshold) but Prestige is long (hard). Gambal's Popularity is easy but Legendary Achievement is steep. Before Round 1 starts, identify your character's 2 hardest thresholds and dedicate your first 2 rounds to those. Rounds 3–4 are for topping off easy thresholds and stacking VP.

#### Privilege Snowball
Privilege (most votes) gives first pick of Council Rewards + 2 VP advances vs. 1 for everyone else. Over 4 rounds, that's up to an 8 VP swing plus consistently better card choices. If you can cheaply accumulate votes via province bonuses or black-row actions, the Privilege advantage compounds.

#### Stratagem Timing
Don't fire all 4 stratagems in Round 1. Bolk's "The Champion" stratagems include a free track advance — save it for Round 3–4 when you need to cross a stubborn threshold. Gambal's bribery stratagems are strongest when opponents have resources worth stealing. Read all 4 stratagems before the game and plan when each has maximum impact.

#### Timeless Mode as Analysis Tool
Even experienced players benefit from playing Timeless Mode occasionally. It lets you study action space values, province card interactions, and track advancement costs without time pressure. Insights from Timeless Mode directly improve your real-time decision speed.

rulings: Timer flipping is voluntary — no rule forces any player to flip a timer. Strategic delay is legal and intended.
Workers are placed on rows where the timer is NOT present. Actions resolve when the timer returns (is flipped onto) that row.
The black section has unlimited worker capacity — any number of workers from any players can share a space there.
A grande worker CAN be placed on an occupied space. A regular worker CANNOT be placed on a space with a grande worker (except in the black section).
A round ends when the purple (3-minute) timer has been flipped 3 times, lasting approximately 9 minutes of real time.
Province cards gained during an action do NOT trigger during that same action — only on future activations of that color slot.
To be eligible to win, ALL 4 track markers (Power, Prestige, Popularity, Legendary Achievement) must be past their threshold lines into the parchment zone.
Each character has different track lengths — threshold distances vary per character, creating asymmetric difficulty profiles.
